name: Amazon SQS API
what_it_does: Amazon Simple Queue Service (SQS) is a fully managed message queuing service offering standard (at-least-once) and FIFO (exactly-once, ordered) queues with dead-letter queue support for reliable async task processing.
use_cases: Queuing agent tasks for asynchronous processing with automatic retry and backoff via visibility timeout
Implementing FIFO task queues to ensure ordered, exactly-once processing in multi-agent pipelines
Routing failed agent tasks to dead-letter queues for inspection, reprocessing, or alerting
Decoupling producer agents from consumer agents to allow independent scaling and failure isolation
Buffering bursts of work from upstream agents so downstream processors can consume at a sustainable rate
not_for: Pub/sub fan-out to multiple consumers simultaneously (use SNS for that pattern)
Message retention longer than 14 days without external archival
Real-time streaming where consumers need sub-millisecond latency
best_when: An agent needs reliable, durable task queuing with built-in retry semantics, dead-letter handling, and the ability to control processing rate within AWS infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need broadcast semantics where the same message must be consumed by multiple independent subscribers without explicit routing.

auth.notes: AWS Signature Version 4 authentication via IAM. Fine-grained IAM policies control actions per queue ARN (sqs:SendMessage, sqs:ReceiveMessage, sqs:DeleteMessage). IAM roles eliminate credential management for EC2/ECS/Lambda agents.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 1 million requests free per month (standard and FIFO queues combined)
pricing.paid_tiers: Standard queues: $0.40 per million requests after free tier. FIFO queues: $0.50 per million requests. Data transfer costs apply for cross-region use.
pricing.notes: Costs are very low for most agent workloads. A single 64KB payload counts as one request; larger payloads (up to 256KB) are billed in 64KB chunks.

performance.rate_limits: Standard queues: nearly unlimited throughput. FIFO queues: 3,000 messages/second with batching (300 without). Long polling reduces empty receive costs.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Consistent XML/JSON error structure with specific error codes (QueueDoesNotExist, InvalidMessageContents, etc.) and unique request IDs. Error taxonomy is well-documented for automated handling.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: FIFO queues support exactly-once processing via message deduplication IDs (5-minute deduplication window). Standard queues are at-least-once only. Message group IDs enable per-group ordering in FIFO.
known_agent_gotchas: Visibility timeout must be set longer than the maximum expected processing time; expiring before deletion causes redelivery and duplicate processing
ReceiveMessage returns at most 10 messages per call; agents must loop to drain a queue, adding latency for high-volume scenarios
Long polling (WaitTimeSeconds > 0) is strongly recommended to reduce empty receives and costs, but requires client-side timeout handling
FIFO queue throughput limits (300-3000 TPS) can bottleneck high-throughput agents; horizontal scaling requires multiple message group IDs
Message attribute type system is limited (String, Number, Binary); complex structured payloads should be serialized to String and parsed by consumers

security_score_components.security_notes: IAM fine-grained access control per queue and per action. SSE with AWS KMS for encryption at rest. VPC endpoints available for private connectivity without internet traversal. Resource-based queue policies for cross-account access.
